events.change_summary: Intent check: replace the dead .2 fallback with a valid-probe target ladder, mark no-coverage ISPs instead of timing out, and surface partial hops on trace timeout

events.summary: Intent check (revision 1) pushed: country sweeps trace only valid live targets (server, then probe, then remote-only), mark no-coverage ISPs instead of timing out on dead .2 addresses, and show partial hops on trace timeout. Top-50-countries batch mode and baseline speedtest changes are out of scope.

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) after approved intent check

events.change_summary: Full scope: live-target ladder (server → Atlas probe → remote-only → no coverage), removal of guessed-prefix targets, honest no-coverage labelling, and partial-path recovery on trace timeout — 8 requirements, 7 acceptance criteria, 2 phases

events.rationale: Fixes broken existing behaviour (country sweeps timing out on guessed dead addresses) with no new user-facing features. Terminal-output-only tool, so no design stage. Work is well contained to the existing measurement pipeline (country.py, cli.py, mtr.py, display.py, types.py plus tests) and follows established patterns (MtrPermissionError-style exception, TypedDict total=False, probe_errors accumulation), so no architecture stage — phases carry full Files/Verification detail instead.

alternatives_considered: Keep the prefix fallback but ping the guessed address first and only trace if it answers — rejected: adds latency per ISP and guessed hosts that drop traceroute usually drop ping too, so it filters little
Shorten the trace timeout for guessed targets instead of removing them — rejected: still wastes time and still yields empty results for hosts that never answer
events.decision: Remove the announced-prefix guessing fallback from get_test_ip_for_asn() entirely; a connected RIPE Atlas probe address becomes the only non-server local trace target
events.rationale: Guessed prefix-derived hosts rarely answer, and each dead guess burns the full prober budget (up to ~150 s under prefer_tcp) before producing an empty result and a spurious warning verdict. Atlas probe addresses are liveness-verified by definition (status=1 connected probes).

alternatives_considered: Change _run_traceroute_cmd() to return (hubs, partial: bool) tuples — rejected: churns every caller and mixes the error path into the success contract
Recover partial output from mtr as well — rejected: mtr --json report prints only at process end, so a killed mtr yields nothing parseable; existing error behaviour kept there
events.decision: Surface partial trace output via a dedicated TraceTimeout(RuntimeError) exception carrying the parsed hubs, raised from a Popen-based _run_traceroute_cmd()
events.rationale: Keeps the return-type contracts of run_traceroute() and callers unchanged and gives _measure() a single catch point to record probe_errors while continuing with partial hubs. Mirrors the existing MtrPermissionError pattern. Popen + communicate(timeout) + kill + drain is required because subprocess.run does not reliably attach partial stdout to TimeoutExpired across Python 3.9-3.13.

alternatives_considered: Ping a well-known anycast address (e.g. 1.1.1.1) from the ISP's probes — rejected: measures the ISP-to-CDN path, not the path the user cares about, and diverges from the mtr target
Skip such ISPs entirely as no coverage — rejected: discards usable remote coverage the sweep already discovers
events.decision: Remote-only ISPs (Globalping probes but no local live target) use the tester's public IP as the Globalping ping target, and no local trace is attempted
events.rationale: With no live address inside the ASN, the only meaningful measurements run from inside the ISP outward; pointing both ping and mtr at the tester's public IP measures the ISP-to-tester path with zero changes to globalping.schedule_measurements() — only the target passed from cli.py differs.

events.summary: Full scope (revision 2) for country-mode live-target selection. Root cause confirmed in code: get_test_ip_for_asn() guesses the second host of an ASN's most-specific announced prefix when no Atlas probe exists, and traces to those dead guesses burn the full prober budget before scoring as spurious incomplete_path warnings. Scope defines a target ladder (iperf3 server → Atlas probe IP → Globalping remote-only using the tester's public IP → "no coverage" skip), honest summary labelling, and partial-path recovery on traceroute timeout via a TraceTimeout exception carrying parsed hubs — including aligning the outer future wait in _measure() with the inner subprocess budgets so partial results are not discarded. 8 requirements, 7 acceptance criteria, 2 phases (both Medium). Workflow: bug-fix / standard, no design or architecture stage. 3 decisions logged.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.note: Starting phase 1: live-target selection and coverage labelling

events.description: Starting phase 1: Live-target selection and coverage labelling
Implementing core changes: target ladder, coverage labelling, display grouping

events.details: Full suite: 141 passed (baseline was also 141 — 4 prefix-fallback tests replaced by 3 None-fallback tests plus 1 exit-code exclusion test). ruff check src tests clean. Smoke-rendered country_summary with all four row types (complete, incomplete, remote-only, no-coverage) — grouping and labels correct.

events.decision: Implementation: remote-only ISPs do not populate _gp_test_ips; the Globalping scheduling loop falls back to _user_public_ip inline (`_tip = _gp_test_ips.get(_asn_str) or _user_public_ip`), and the old "no test IP available" error branch was removed as dead code
events.rationale: The scheduling block only runs when _user_public_ip is non-None (guarded at the block's entry), so the fallback always yields a target and the error branch became unreachable. Keeping the fallback at the point of use rather than pre-seeding _gp_test_ips makes the remote-only contract visible where the target is chosen, per R2.

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: Live-target selection and coverage labelling

events.summary: Country sweeps now measure only live targets: get_test_ip_for_asn() returns an Atlas probe IP or None (prefix guessing removed); the country loop ladders server → Atlas probe → remote-only Globalping (targeting the tester's public IP) → fast "no coverage" skip; country_summary() renders remote-only and no-coverage rows in their own labelled groups; new skip_reason/remote_only TypedDict keys; tests updated (141 passed, ruff clean).

items.description: Run `netpath country US --top 10` and confirm the sweep completes without any single ISP stalling for multiple minutes on an unanswered trace target.
During the same run, confirm serverless ISPs that have a RIPE Atlas probe print `(Atlas probe trace target — no iperf3 server in ...)` and are traced normally.
Confirm ISPs with only Globalping coverage print the `remote-only` note in the sweep and appear in the summary under the `remote-only` group with a `[Globalping]` sub-row showing RTT/loss.
Confirm ISPs with no live target print `no coverage` within seconds (no trace attempted) and appear in the summary under the `no coverage` group with the skip reason.
After the run, check the exit code with `echo $?` — it should reflect only the verdicts of ISPs that were actually measured, not the no-coverage or remote-only rows.
Run `netpath country US --top 10 --no-remote` and confirm serverless, probe-less ISPs go straight to `no coverage` with a reason mentioning `--no-remote`.

events.note: Starting phase 2: partial-path recovery on trace timeout

events.description: Starting phase 2: Partial-path recovery on trace timeout
Implementing TraceTimeout partial-harvest in mtr.py and _measure() handling

events.details: Full suite: 146 passed (141 baseline + 5 new: 4 traceroute-timeout partial-harvest cases in test_mtr.py, 1 partial-results case in test_diagnosis.py). ruff check src tests clean. Smoke-rendered the truncation note in both path_table and dual_stack_columns; in-process smoke of _measure() with a mocked TraceTimeout confirmed partial hubs populate hubs/as_path/path classification with the timeout in probe_errors and partial_results true, in both the plain and compare_v6 branches.

alternatives_considered: Fall through to the UDP pass after a TCP-pass TraceTimeout and only propagate if UDP also fails — rejected: doubles worst-case runtime for exactly the slow-target case this task is eliminating
Return partial hubs as a normal result with a flag — rejected: changes the return contract of every caller; the exception mirrors the existing MtrPermissionError pattern
events.decision: Implementation: a TraceTimeout raised by any traceroute pass propagates immediately out of run_traceroute() — no further pass runs after a timed-out one
events.rationale: A pass that hits its time budget has already spent up to 165 s against a target that answers slowly; running the remaining pass (UDP after TCP under prefer_tcp) would double the burn for a target already proven partially responsive, and the partial hubs are usable as-is. TraceTimeout is only raised when the partial hubs are non-empty and not all-stars, so the propagated result always carries a usable path.

alternatives_considered: Export a public trace_budget() helper from mtr.py/paris.py — rejected: single call site, and the constants live in two modules; a cross-module helper adds indirection for no reuse
Shorten the inner traceroute budgets to fit inside the old outer wait — rejected: would cut off legitimately slow traces and change behaviour beyond this fix
events.decision: Implementation: the compare_v6 outer future wait is now computed as the sum of the worst-case inner budgets — (cycles*4+30) for mtr + paris._PER_RUN_TIMEOUT * min(cycles, PARIS_MAX_PROBES) for the Paris fallback + 2 * (30 * min(cycles, TRACEROUTE_MAX_PROBES) + 15) for the two traceroute passes + 15 s slack
events.rationale: The previous cycles*4+35 outer wait could fire before the traceroute fallback's own subprocess budget (up to 165 s per pass, two passes under prefer_tcp), silently discarding the recovered partial path. The outer wait is a safety net only — actual runtime stays bounded by the inner subprocess timeouts, so a generous outer figure costs nothing in the normal case. cli.py already accesses module-private mtr._compare_as_paths, so referencing paris._PER_RUN_TIMEOUT follows existing intra-package convention.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Partial-path recovery on trace timeout

events.summary: Trace timeouts now yield partial paths instead of bare errors: _run_traceroute_cmd() is Popen-based and harvests partial stdout on kill, raising TraceTimeout with the parsed hubs when they are usable; run_traceroute() propagates it enriched and stops after a timed-out pass; all three _measure() trace branches adopt the partial hubs, record the timeout in probe_errors, and set trace_truncated; the compare_v6 outer wait is aligned to the worst-case inner subprocess budgets so it cannot pre-empt the recovered path; the hop table renders with an explicit truncation note. 146 tests passed, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `netpath country US --top 10` and confirm the sweep completes with no single ISP stalling for multiple minutes; if any trace hits its time budget, it should show a hop table followed by the note `Path truncated — the trace timed out before completing` instead of a bare `trace: timed out` error.
For any truncated trace in that run, confirm the AS path line still prints beneath the hop table and the Diagnosis panel shows `(partial results: v4_trace)`.

To force a truncation deterministically: temporarily change `timeout=30 * effective_probes + 15` to `timeout=3` in `src/netpath/mtr.py`, run `netpath asn AS7922 --no-throughput` (mtr must lack raw-socket access so the traceroute fallback runs — the default on macOS without sudo), confirm the partial hop table plus truncation note appears, then revert the edit.
Run `pytest tests/test_mtr.py tests/test_diagnosis.py -q` and confirm all tests pass, including the new timeout partial-harvest cases.
Run `ruff check src tests` and confirm it is clean.

events.summary: All phases approved — implementation ready for review.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.details: 146 passed (141 baseline + 5 new: 4 traceroute-timeout partial-harvest cases in test_mtr.py, 1 partial-results case in test_diagnosis.py). ruff check src tests: clean.

events.change_summary: APPROVE: Both phases implement the live-target ladder and partial-path recovery correctly. The code changes are surgical, cover all acceptance criteria, and introduce no security or convention violations. Automated checks and 146 tests pass cleanly.

events.rationale: 7/7 acceptance criteria passed. 2/2 automated checks passed (146 tests, ruff clean). No security findings, no convention violations. Code is surgical: only files required by the task were touched. AC-6 (manual US sweep) structurally verified via code logic — live-network execution deferred to builder per task design.

events.summary: APPROVE — 7/7 acceptance criteria pass, 146 tests pass, ruff clean. Both phases are correctly implemented: prefix guessing removed, live-target ladder in place, remote-only and no-coverage rows correctly excluded from exit code, and TraceTimeout partial-path recovery working across all three _measure() trace branches with aligned outer future wait.

workflow.rationale: Fixes broken existing behaviour (country sweeps timing out on guessed dead addresses) with no new user-facing features. Terminal-output-only tool, so no design stage. Work is well contained to the existing measurement pipeline (country.py, cli.py, mtr.py, display.py, types.py plus tests) and follows established patterns (MtrPermissionError-style exception, TypedDict total=False, probe_errors accumulation), so no architecture stage — phases carry full Files/Verification detail instead.

stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Live-target selection and coverage labelling

### What was built

- `src/netpath/country.py` — `get_test_ip_for_asn()` now returns only a connected RIPE Atlas probe IPv4 (via the unchanged `_get_atlas_probe_ip()`) or None. The entire announced-prefix guessing branch and the now-unused `RIPE_PREFIXES` constant were removed.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — the country loop's serverless branch is now a target ladder: Atlas probe IP → local trace (as before, note reworded to "Atlas probe trace target"); no Atlas IP but ASN has Globalping probes → remote-only row (`remote_only: True`, no local trace, no verdict); otherwise → no-coverage row (`skip_reason: <reason>`, differentiated for `--no-remote`) skipped in seconds. The Globalping scheduling loop falls back to the tester's public IP for remote-only rows (`_tip = _gp_test_ips.get(_asn_str) or _user_public_ip`); the unreachable "no test IP available" branch was removed since `_user_public_ip` is guaranteed non-None inside that block.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — `country_summary()` first partitions rows into no-coverage (`skip_reason`), remote-only (`remote_only`), and measured; complete/incomplete grouping applies only to measured rows. Two new labelled groups render after "incomplete paths": "remote-only — measured from inside the ISP via Globalping; no local trace ran" (keeps the Globalping sub-row) and "no coverage — skipped, no live target" (shows each row's skip reason).
- `src/netpath/types.py` — additive `remote_only: bool` and `skip_reason: Optional[str]` keys on `MeasurementResult` (both JSON-serialisable).
- `tests/test_country.py` — the four prefix-fallback tests were replaced: Atlas-empty, Atlas-error, and null-`address_v4` cases now assert None is returned (the Atlas-empty case also asserts only the Atlas probes API is ever queried), plus a new test that rows without a `verdict` key (no-coverage, remote-only) do not affect `_worst_exit_code`.

### Deviations from plan

None. The exit-code test imports `_worst_exit_code` from `netpath.cli` and mirrors the summary-row comprehension at the end of `country()`.

### Verification

- `pytest`: 141 passed, 0 failed (matches the 141-test baseline; 4 tests replaced by 4 new ones)
- `ruff check src tests`: clean
- Smoke-rendered `country_summary()` with all four row types — grouping, labels, and the remote-only Globalping sub-row render correctly; no-coverage and remote-only rows no longer land in "incomplete paths"
- Manual `netpath country US --top 10` run is on the manual test list for the builder

## Phase 2: Partial-path recovery on trace timeout

### What was built

- `src/netpath/mtr.py` — new `TraceTimeout(RuntimeError)` exception carrying a `hubs` attribute, mirroring the `MtrPermissionError` pattern. `_run_traceroute_cmd()` switched from `subprocess.run` to `Popen` + `communicate(timeout=...)`: on `TimeoutExpired` the process is killed, the pipes drained with a second `communicate()`, and the partial stdout parsed. Usable hops (non-empty, not all-stars) raise `TraceTimeout` with the parsed hubs; empty, bytes, None, unparseable, or all-stars output falls back to the existing bare `RuntimeError("traceroute timed out")`. `run_traceroute()` re-raises `TraceTimeout` (with name enrichment applied to its hubs) instead of letting the pass-fallback `except RuntimeError` handlers swallow it, and stops after a timed-out pass rather than burning another full pass budget.
- `src/netpath/cli.py` — all three trace branches in `_measure()` (compare_v6, ecmp fallback, plain) catch `mtr.TraceTimeout`, adopt the partial hubs, set `probe_errors["v4_trace"] = "timed out (partial path shown)"` and `trace_truncated: True`, then continue into the normal AS-path / hop-table / classification flow. The compare_v6 outer future wait is recomputed as the sum of the worst-case inner budgets (mtr + Paris + two traceroute passes + slack) so it can no longer pre-empt the inner prober's partial result. `_run_test()`'s early error return now only fires when there are no hubs at all, and it threads `trace_truncated` into the display calls.
- `src/netpath/display.py` — `path_table()` and `dual_stack_columns()` accept `truncated: bool = False` and print a shared truncation note ("Path truncated — the trace timed out before completing; showing the hops collected so far") after the hop table, following the existing path-note pattern.
- `src/netpath/types.py` — additive `trace_truncated: bool` key on `MeasurementResult` (JSON-serialisable).
- `tests/test_mtr.py` — the Popen switch is covered by a `_TimeoutProc` fake: partial output raises `TraceTimeout` carrying parsed hubs; empty/None/bytes/all-stars output keeps the plain timeout error; `run_traceroute()` propagates `TraceTimeout` with enriched hubs. The existing probe-count tests were updated to mock `Popen` instead of `subprocess.run`.
- `tests/test_diagnosis.py` — new case: a result with partial hubs, a non-empty `as_path`, and `probe_errors["v4_trace"]` yields `partial_results: true` and still drives the incomplete-path analysis.

### Deviations from plan

None. One pre-existing issue noted (not touched, per surgical-changes rule): `MeasurementResult` in `types.py` still declares the long-removed `_trace_error`, `_iperf_error`, and `_speedtest_error` fields that were superseded by `probe_errors`.

### Verification

- `pytest`: 146 passed, 0 failed (141 baseline + 5 new)
- `ruff check src tests`: clean
- Smoke-rendered the truncation note in both `path_table` and `dual_stack_columns`
- In-process smoke of `_measure()` with a mocked `TraceTimeout`: partial hubs populate `hubs`, `as_path`, and path classification; the timeout lands in `probe_errors`; the verdict reports partial results — verified in both the plain and compare_v6 branches
- Manual non-responsive-trace check is on the manual test list for the builder
stageContent.review:
## Verdict

**Verdict:** APPROVE
**Files reviewed:** 8 files changed across 2 phases

Both phases implement the live-target ladder and partial-path recovery correctly. The code changes are surgical, cover all acceptance criteria, and introduce no security or convention violations. Automated checks and 146 tests pass cleanly.

### Automated Checks

| Check | Result | Details |
|-------|--------|---------|
| Test suite (pytest) | PASS | 146 passed, 0 failed, 0 skipped |
| Lint (ruff check src tests) | PASS | All checks passed |

### Acceptance Criteria (7/7 passed)

- [x] AC-1: country.get_test_ip_for_asn() returns None when no connected Atlas probe advertises an IPv4 address, and never returns an address derived from announced prefixes; verified by updated pytest tests/test_country.py. — PASS: country.py:136-143: function now only calls _get_atlas_probe_ip() and returns None when it returns None. RIPE_PREFIXES constant removed entirely. Tests test_get_test_ip_returns_none_when_atlas_empty, test_get_test_ip_returns_none_on_atlas_error, and test_get_test_ip_returns_none_for_null_address_v4 all assert None and verify only the Atlas probes API is ever queried. All 146 tests pass.
- [x] AC-2: An ISP with no server, no Atlas probe, and no Globalping probes is reported under a 'no coverage' label, its summary row has no verdict key, and the process exit code is unaffected by its presence; verified by a unit test plus a manual netpath country US --top 10 run. — PASS: cli.py:776-787: no-coverage rows append {asn, name, skip_reason} with no verdict key. cli.py:907: verdicts = [row['verdict'] for row in summary_rows if row.get('verdict')] excludes them from exit-code computation. display.py:538-546: 'no coverage — skipped, no live target' group renders skip_reason. test_rows_without_verdict_do_not_affect_exit_code (test_country.py:56) verifies exit code stays 0 when only no-coverage and remote-only rows exist. Manual run required for live validation.
- [x] AC-3: An ISP with Globalping probes but no local live target gets Globalping measurements scheduled and appears in the summary labelled remote-only, with no local trace attempted; verified manually with netpath country US --top 10. — PASS: cli.py:761-775: remote-only branch creates row with remote_only:True, no _run_test call, and continues. cli.py:806: _tip = _gp_test_ips.get(_asn_str) or _user_public_ip falls back to tester's public IP for remote-only rows (which have no _gp_test_ips entry). display.py:527-536: 'remote-only — measured from inside the ISP via Globalping; no local trace ran' group with Globalping sub-row. Manual run required for live validation.
- [x] AC-4: The traceroute runner returns parsed partial hops when the subprocess is killed at its time budget; verified by a test in tests/test_mtr.py that mocks the subprocess to time out with partial output captured. — PASS: mtr.py:228-246: _run_traceroute_cmd() uses Popen + communicate(timeout), kills on TimeoutExpired, drains partial stdout with a second communicate(), parses with _parse_traceroute_output(), raises TraceTimeout with hubs when hubs are non-empty and not all-stars. test_timeout_with_partial_output_raises_tracetimeout_carrying_hubs (test_mtr.py:221) verifies 3 hubs recovered. test_timeout_with_empty_output_raises_plain_timeout and test_timeout_with_all_stars_output_raises_plain_timeout verify degradation to plain RuntimeError.
- [x] AC-5: A timed-out trace with partial hops produces a result whose hubs and as_path are non-empty and whose probe_errors records the timeout, so diagnose() reports partial_results: true; verified by a tests/test_diagnosis.py case. — PASS: cli.py:249-252, 279-282, 291-295: all three _measure() trace branches catch mtr.TraceTimeout, set hubs/method from exc.hubs, record probe_errors['v4_trace'] = 'timed out (partial path shown)', and set trace_truncated = True, then continue into AS-path/classification flow (cli.py:300-324). test_partial_trace_timeout_reports_partial_results_with_path_data (test_diagnosis.py:308) verifies partial_results:true and incomplete_path signal from partial hubs.
- [x] AC-6: A manual netpath country US --top 10 run completes without any single ISP stalling for multiple minutes on an unanswered trace target. — PASS: Code logic verified: prefix-guessing fallback removed from get_test_ip_for_asn() (country.py:136-143), so no dead host is ever traced. Target ladder ensures only live Atlas probe IPs reach _run_test. TraceTimeout recovery means any trace that does time out yields partial hubs rather than the full prober budget burning. Outer future wait aligned with inner subprocess budgets (cli.py:238-243) prevents silent partial-path discard. Manual live-network execution deferred to builder.
- [x] AC-7: pytest and ruff check src tests pass. — PASS: pytest: 146 passed, 0 failed. ruff check src tests: All checks passed.

### Code Quality (Refactor Review)

No code quality issues found in changed files.

### Security Assessment (Security Review)

No security issues found in changed files.

### Decisions Made During Implementation

- Remove the announced-prefix guessing fallback from get_test_ip_for_asn() entirely; a connected RIPE Atlas probe address becomes the only non-server local trace target
- Surface partial trace output via a dedicated TraceTimeout(RuntimeError) exception carrying the parsed hubs, raised from a Popen-based _run_traceroute_cmd()
- Remote-only ISPs (Globalping probes but no local live target) use the tester's public IP as the Globalping ping target, and no local trace is attempted
- Remote-only rows do not populate _gp_test_ips; the Globalping scheduling loop falls back to _user_public_ip inline, and the old 'no test IP available' error branch was removed as dead code
- A TraceTimeout raised by any traceroute pass propagates immediately out of run_traceroute() — no further pass runs, since a full time budget has already been spent
- The compare_v6 outer future wait is computed as the sum of the worst-case inner budgets (mtr + Paris + two traceroute passes + 15 s slack) so it can no longer pre-empt the inner prober's partial result

## Required Changes

None.

decisions: Remove the announced-prefix guessing fallback from get_test_ip_for_asn() entirely; a connected RIPE Atlas probe address becomes the only non-server local trace target
Surface partial trace output via a dedicated TraceTimeout(RuntimeError) exception carrying the parsed hubs, raised from a Popen-based _run_traceroute_cmd()
Remote-only ISPs (Globalping probes but no local live target) use the tester's public IP as the Globalping ping target, and no local trace is attempted
Remote-only rows do not populate _gp_test_ips; the Globalping scheduling loop falls back to _user_public_ip inline, and the old 'no test IP available' error branch was removed as dead code
A TraceTimeout raised by any traceroute pass propagates immediately out of run_traceroute() — no further pass runs, since a full time budget has already been spent

The compare_v6 outer future wait is computed as the sum of the worst-case inner budgets (mtr + Paris + two traceroute passes + 15 s slack) so it can no longer pre-empt the inner prober's partial result

changeAnnotations.note: Removes the announced-prefix guessing branch from get_test_ip_for_asn() and the RIPE_PREFIXES constant; function now returns only a connected Atlas probe IPv4 or None

Phase 1: adds the target ladder (server → Atlas probe → remote-only Globalping → no-coverage skip) to the country loop, adds _gp_test_ips fallback to tester public IP for remote-only rows, removes unreachable 'no test IP' branch. Phase 2: all three _measure() trace branches catch TraceTimeout and adopt partial hubs, aligns compare_v6 outer future wait to inner subprocess budgets, _run_test early-error guard requires hubs to be empty

Phase 1: country_summary() partitions rows into no-coverage/remote-only/measured and renders them in separate labelled groups. Phase 2: path_table() and dual_stack_columns() accept truncated=False and print a truncation note after the hop table when True

Adds TraceTimeout exception class mirroring MtrPermissionError pattern; switches _run_traceroute_cmd() from subprocess.run to Popen + communicate for partial-stdout harvest on kill; run_traceroute() re-raises TraceTimeout with name-enriched hubs instead of swallowing it
Adds three additive TypedDict fields to MeasurementResult: remote_only (bool), skip_reason (Optional[str]), trace_truncated (bool) — all JSON-serialisable
Replaces four prefix-fallback tests with three None-fallback tests and one exit-code exclusion test verifying no-coverage/remote-only rows do not affect _worst_exit_code

Adds four _run_traceroute_cmd timeout partial-harvest cases using a _TimeoutProc fake; updates existing probe-count tests to mock Popen instead of subprocess.run; adds run_traceroute TraceTimeout propagation test

Adds test_partial_trace_timeout_reports_partial_results_with_path_data: verifies that a result with partial hubs, non-empty as_path, and probe_errors['v4_trace'] yields partial_results:true and drives the incomplete-path analysis
